channel.title: Anime Architecture Archive
channel.description: Fictional Universe Intelligence System — power mechanics, factions, and system analysis for anime worlds.

item.title: Tokyo Ghoul
item.description: Tokyo Ghoul — A predator-prey economy where the ghoul/human boundary shapes every choice.

item.title: Tokyo Revengers
item.description: Tokyo Revengers — A time-leaping delinquent rewrites futures by changing his own choices.

item.title: Vinland Saga
item.description: Vinland Saga: A deterministic economy of retributive violence

item.title: Spy x Family
item.description: Spy x Family — A spy, an assassin, and a telepath form a fake family — and somehow grow to mean it.

item.title: Steins;Gate
item.description: Steins;Gate: A deterministic attractor-field prison where convergence can be controlled

item.title: Sword Art Online
item.description: Sword Art Online — 10,000 players trapped. Only victory or death exists as options.

item.title: One Punch Man
item.description: One Punch Man — a hero defeats everything with one punch, comedically breaking the entire power-scaling economy.

item.title: Parasyte
item.description: Parasyte — A parasite takes over your right hand and becomes your unlikely partner in survival.

item.title: Re:Zero - Starting Life in Another World
item.description: Re:Zero — Save-scum your way to victory if you die enough times to find the winning timeline.

item.title: Solo Leveling
item.description: Solo Leveling — One hunter shatters a fixed-rank system by running an infinite growth protocol.

item.title: Mushoku Tensei: Jobless Reincarnation
item.description: Mushoku Tensei — Mana, bloodlines, and looping causality drive reincarnation-powered fate.

item.title: Naruto
item.description: Naruto — Shinobi navigate political loyalty and bloodlines, but combat mastery defines the hero.

item.title: One Piece
item.description: One Piece — Maritime empires chase ancient secrets where alliance networks become supreme power.

item.title: Hunter x Hunter
item.description: Hunter x Hunter: Contractual Power Economy & Asymmetric Information Warfare

item.title: Jujutsu Kaisen
item.description: Jujutsu Kaisen: Negative Energy Economy & Algorithmic Combat

item.title: My Hero Academia
item.description: My Hero Academia — Mutating superpowers collide with state hero capitalism as society crumbles.

item.title: Mob Psycho 100
item.description: Mob Psycho 100 — A powerful psychic navigates supernatural threats while mastering his own emotions.

item.title: Fullmetal Alchemist: Brotherhood
item.description: Fullmetal Alchemist — Every creation demands equal destruction in a rigged thermodynamic arms race.

item.title: Sousou no Frieren
item.description: Sousou no Frieren — An elf mage battles the demon king, then faces the melancholy of outliving everyone she loved.

item.title: Goblin Slayer
item.description: Goblin Slayer — A low-prestige extermination specialist proves that heroism isn't about power levels — it's about purpose.

item.title: Demon Slayer: Kimetsu no Yaiba
item.description: Demon Slayer — Demon slayers burn their lifespan to unleash solar power against immortal demons.

item.title: Dragon Ball Z
item.description: Dragon Ball Z — Ki warriors unlock exponential transformation tiers to determine who destroys what.

item.title: Fire Force
item.description: Fire Force — spontaneous combustion turns humans into fire monsters. Company 8 fights to stop the flames and save Tokyo.

item.title: Death Note
item.description: Death Note — An underground executioner vs. the world's greatest detective in a deadly names game.

item.title: Bleach
item.description: Bleach — Soul Reapers wield Zanpakuto where Reiatsu dominance determines survival across worlds.

item.title: Blue Lock
item.description: Blue Lock — 300 strikers enter a facility. Only one becomes the egoist striker the world needs.

item.title: Chainsaw Man
item.description: Chainsaw Man — A fear-fed devil economy where chainsaw demons rewrite the rules of power.

item.title: Code Geass: Hangyaku no Lelouch
item.description: Code Geass — A resistance leader with mind-control Geass wages war against an empire.

item.title: Black Clover
item.description: Black Clover — Anti-magic versus elemental sovereignty as willpower bets against bloodline.

item.title: Attack on Titan
item.description: Attack on Titan: A brutal deterministic closed-loop temporal matrix

item.title: The Determinism Engine in Attack on Titan
item.description: Why every freedom fight circles back to the same trap.

item.title: Why Devil Contracts Are a Raw Deal
item.description: Fear as fuel, trauma as currency.

item.title: Death Note as a Tool Critique
item.description: The notebook as a mirror for power fantasies.

item.title: Breathing Forms as Weaponized Poetry
item.description: How swordsmanship became a language system.

item.title: Why Nen Is the Best Magic System in Anime
item.description: Vow and Limitations as a self-limiting growth protocol.

item.title: Cursed Energy as an Inverse Heat Engine
item.description: Why Gojo's Infinity works like a thermodynamic paradox.

item.title: Chakra as a Bureaucratic Resource
item.description: How shinobi villages turned personal energy into a labor market.

item.title: Why the One Piece World Is Designed Like an Economy
item.description: The Yonko system, maritime trade routes, and Devil Fruit markets.

item.title: The Most Complex Anime Power Systems, Ranked by Structure
item.description: We ranked anime power systems not by flashiness, but by structural depth. From Nen to cursed energy to titan shifting — which system is the most architecturally complex?

item.title: Best Anime Worldbuilding: 5 Universes Where the World Is the Main Character
item.description: Some anime have worlds so structurally rich that the setting itself drives the story. These 5 universes have the most architecturally complete worldbuilding in anime.

item.title: LORE vs SYS Mode: How the Archive Sees Anime Differently
item.description: Every universe in the archive can be read two ways: as a story (LORE) or as an engineered system (SYS). Here's why that matters and how to use it.
